title: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild
edition_pinned: The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild (Nintendo Switch / Wii U, 2017) — not Tears of the Kingdom; chemistry+physics open-world rules as shipped at launch
moat: Shared chemistry+physics state rules let any object rewrite routes, combat, and puzzles without scripted solutions.

loop_skeleton.description: Survey terrain, commit stamina to a climb or glider path, apply weapons and elemental states to enemies or materials, cook or craft for the next push, clear a shrine or objective that refunds a new option into the open field.

depth_mechanisms.name: chemistry engine
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: A rule-based state calculator tracks material/element states (fire, ice, electricity, wind, water) so elements change material states and each other; materials do not change each other. The same rules drive puzzles, combat, and traversal.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If chemistry states are removed and only rigid scripted puzzle flags remain, shrine clear rates fall and players stop inventing off-script combat/route solutions after session one.

depth_mechanisms.name: weapon durability budget
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Weapons break after a finite number of hits, so inventory slots become temporary tools rather than permanent loadouts; the player must choose when to spend a high-damage weapon and where to restock from the field.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If durability is removed and strongest weapons never break, players stop picking up field weapons and combat diversity collapses to a fixed favorite loadout by session two.

depth_mechanisms.name: stamina-gated vertical traversal
depth_mechanisms.mechanism: Climb and glider use a shared stamina budget; nearly any surface is legal but height and weather punish over-commit, so route choice is a resource decision rather than a pathfinder unlock.
depth_mechanisms.falsifiable_claim: If stamina is infinite on climb/glider, average time-to-first-major-landmark drops and second-session route planning (rest/food/weather) becomes irrelevant.

feel_signature: Each commit spends a measurable resource (stamina, weapon HP, cook ingredients); success is a new route or state interaction the player composed, not a scripted set-piece completion.
second_session_answer: Players return to restock weapons and food, then re-route through known chemistry interactions (e.g. metal+lightning, fire+grass) toward unexplored towers and shrines rather than to grind a skill tree.
packaging_lessons: Ship a small closed test bed of chemistry rules before open-world scale; the GDC panel treats chemistry as a pair to physics, not as decorative VFX.
Make the default tools (climb, glider, breakable weapons) work everywhere before adding special-case dungeon keys.
anti_lessons: Do not copy weapon durability without flooding the world with restock points and alternate tool types — durability alone without abundance only produces inventory anxiety.
Do not treat open world as content volume; without consistent state rules, the map is a checklist of one-off scripts.

system_bom.note: climb/glider/combat/cook cycle must share the same material and stamina rules
weapon/shield/bow slots are temporary budgets that force restock exploration
cooking converts field ingredients into stamina and resistance consumables for the next route
shrines and towers gate new map options without requiring linear story order
world material state and inventory must persist so chemistry experiments carry across sessions
death returns to last save/shrine; failure is lost inventory progress and route commit, not a genre-style game over screen

citations.claim: moat/chemistry: chemistry engine is a rule-based state calculator (elements change materials and each other; materials do not change each other) paired with physics.
depth_mechanisms.chemistry engine: GDC 2017 Breaking Conventions session covers physics+chemistry implementation for player-authored solutions.
citations.source: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QyMsF31NdNc (GDC: Breaking Conventions with The Legend of Zelda: Breath of the Wild)

citations.claim: depth_mechanisms.weapon durability budget: Fujibayashi/Aonuma describe breakable weapons as making swing decisions strategic (when/on whom to spend a weapon).
feel_signature / second_session: experimental solutions emerge from interconnected physics+chemistry rules rather than scripted paths.
packaging_lessons: chemistry framed as state calculator alongside Havok physics for consistent world lies.
system_bom.inventory durability restock pressure as exploration driver.
